procedure_overview: Quadriceps Tendon Repair (soft_tissue_repair). Certified. Score 4. Source-backed from v3.
must_know_anatomy: Quadriceps tendon: 2-4 distinct layers (anterior rectus femoris, middle vastus medialis + vastus lateralis, deep vastus intermedius). Inserts on superior patella pole with retinacular expansions.
Complete rupture: palpable gap, inability to actively extend knee against gravity or straight leg raise. Partial may have intact extension but weakness/pain. MRI/US for confirmation.
Superior pole of patella is the insertion; retinacula (medial/lateral) provide important extensions for repair and stability. Suprapatellar pouch communicates with joint.
Patellofemoral tracking: quad tendon repair affects extensor mechanism and patellar position/tracking on trochlea. Assess after repair.
Sensory: infrapatellar branch of saphenous may be at risk in anterior incisions; protect if encountered.
Repair: transpatellar tunnels or anchors/sutures through tendon layers + retinacula. Chronic may need V-Y or graft augmentation.
surgical_approach_anatomy: Anterior midline or slightly medial incision over the tendon gap and superior patella. Develop subcutaneous plane, protect any saphenous branches. Expose the ruptured tendon ends and superior patella pole.

Repair: freshen tendon ends. Use heavy non-absorbable suture in Krackow or similar through the 2-4 layers. Pass through transpatellar bone tunnels or use anchors/screws in superior patella. Repair retinacula for stability and tracking.
Post-repair: assess tracking on trochlea, tension, and stability. Close in layers. Protect repair post-op (brace, limited flexion initially).

structures_at_risk.why_at_risk: Crosses anterior knee in subcutaneous or retinacular plane; at risk during anterior incision for quad tendon exposure/repair.
structures_at_risk.how_to_avoid_injury: Identify under skin flap if visible; protect or retract medially/laterally. Avoid blind cautery in subcutaneous layer.
structures_at_risk.consequence_of_injury: Anterolateral knee/ patellar numbness or painful neuroma. Tolerable but can be annoying post-op.
structures_at_risk.approach_context: Anterior approach for quadriceps tendon repair

structures_at_risk.why_at_risk: Insertion site for quad tendon; retinacula provide lateral/medial stability. Poor repair leads to tracking issues or re-rupture.
structures_at_risk.how_to_avoid_injury: Freshen bone/tendon interface. Secure multi-layer repair through bone tunnels or anchors. Repair retinacula anatomically for stability and patellar tracking.
structures_at_risk.consequence_of_injury: Extensor lag, patellar maltracking/tilt, re-rupture, or need for revision/augmentation.
structures_at_risk.approach_context: Quad tendon insertion/repair at superior patella

structures_at_risk.why_at_risk: Quad tendon repair affects patellar position and extensor pull; imbalance or overtensioning leads to tilt, subluxation, or pain on trochlea.
structures_at_risk.how_to_avoid_injury: Assess tracking intra-op after repair (through ROM). Avoid overtension; ensure balanced retinacular repair. Check for trochlear dysplasia or other factors.
structures_at_risk.consequence_of_injury: Patellofemoral pain, instability, maltracking, or arthritis. May require revision or realignment procedures.
structures_at_risk.approach_context: Extensor mechanism repair and patellar tracking assessment

structures_at_risk.why_at_risk: Communicates with knee joint; hematoma or infection risk if not managed. Repair must restore extensor continuity without joint contamination issues.
structures_at_risk.how_to_avoid_injury: Control bleeding into pouch. Repair in layers. Post-op wound care and monitor for effusion/infection.
structures_at_risk.consequence_of_injury: Hematoma, stiffness, or septic arthritis if contamination occurs.
structures_at_risk.approach_context: Anterior quad repair near suprapatellar pouch

common_mistakes: Failure to identify/protect infrapatellar saphenous branch in anterior incision, causing annoying numbness or neuroma.
Inadequate multi-layer repair or retinacular repair leading to extensor lag, patellar maltracking, or re-rupture.
Overtensioning or poor tracking assessment after repair, causing patellofemoral pain or instability.
Missing chronic nature or retraction (needs V-Y/graft); early repair has better outcomes.
Ignoring suprapatellar pouch communication (hematoma or infection risk).
attending_pimp_questions: Q: Describe the layers of the quadriceps tendon and why identifying them matters for repair. A: 2-4 layers (ant rectus femoris, mid vastus med + lat, deep vastus int). Identifying allows anatomic multi-layer repair for strength and prevents gapping or weakness.

Q: What is the role of the medial and lateral retinacula in quadriceps tendon repair and patellar tracking? A: Provide important medial/lateral extensions for repair stability. Repairing them restores balanced pull and prevents tilt/subluxation on the trochlea.

Q: How do you assess and protect patellofemoral tracking after quad tendon repair? A: After repair, cycle knee through ROM and observe patellar tracking on trochlea. Repair retinacula anatomically; avoid overtension. Check for dysplasia or other factors if maltracking persists.

Q: What sensory nerve is at risk in anterior approach for quad tendon repair and consequence of injury? A: Infrapatellar branch of saphenous. Injury causes anterolateral knee/patellar numbness or neuroma (tolerable but annoying). Identify/protect under skin flap.

Q: What are options for chronic quadriceps tendon rupture with retraction? A: V-Y advancement, tendon graft augmentation, or transpatellar repair with reinforcement. Early repair is ideal; chronic has higher re-rupture and stiffness risk.
night_before_review_checklist: Review linked modules and source URLs
Confirm any case-specific fluoroscopy or approach details
limitations: Anatomy-focused case prep only; does not replace full surgical technique or attending-specific preferences
Always verify with latest imaging and team briefing

surgical_layers.layer_name: Superficial (skin to quad tendon fascia / retinacula)
surgical_layers.what_user_should_know: Midline or slightly medial anterior incision over the palpable gap and superior patella. Develop subcutaneous flaps, identify/protect infrapatellar saphenous branches if crossing. Expose the ruptured quad tendon and superior patella pole with retinacular extensions.
surgical_layers.surgical_relevance: Standard anterior approach for quad tendon repair. Early sensory nerve protection and full exposure of layers + retinacula are key for multi-layer repair and tracking.

surgical_layers.what_user_should_know: Identify the 2-4 tendon layers (ant RF, mid VM+VL, deep VI). Freshen ends. Repair with heavy suture (Krackow or similar) through layers. Secure to superior patella via transpatellar tunnels or anchors. Repair retinacula for lateral/medial stability and patellar tracking on trochlea.
surgical_layers.surgical_relevance: The repair layer. Multi-layer anatomic repair + retinacula is essential for strength, tracking, and preventing re-rupture or maltracking.

surgical_layers.what_user_should_know: After repair, assess patellar tracking through full ROM on trochlea. Close in layers (fascia if possible, subcutaneous, skin). Protect repair post-op (brace in extension or limited flexion, gradual ROM). Monitor for stiffness or tracking issues.
surgical_layers.surgical_relevance: Closure layer confirms tracking and protects the repair. Early protected motion balances healing vs stiffness.

validation_warnings: Quadriceps tendon repair payload is anterior approach focused on layers, insertion, and tracking. Patellar tendon rupture is separate (inferior pole). Source material previously contained TKA/ACL/meniscus bleed, stringified/empty SAR, generic, and placeholders; cleaned to public 3023 facts and rich 6-field content only.
danger_zones: Infrapatellar saphenous branch: identify/protect in anterior subcutaneous; injury causes knee numbness/neuroma.
Superior patella pole + retinacula: secure multi-layer repair; poor repair causes lag, maltracking, or re-rupture.
Patellofemoral tracking on trochlea: assess after repair; imbalance causes pain/instability/arthritis.
Suprapatellar pouch: control bleeding/contamination; infection or stiffness risk if issues.
approach_specific_notes: This payload is for quadriceps tendon repair (acute or chronic). Focus on 2-4 tendon layers, superior patella insertion, retinacular repair for tracking/stability, and sensory nerve protection. No patellar tendon rupture, TKA, ACL, or meniscus content as primary (comparative notes only if in validation). Content is extensor mechanism-specific at quad level.
